patent_context.patent_title: Automated greenhouse ventilation system with temperature feedback
patent_context.claim_description: An automated greenhouse ventilation system comprising a temperature sensor array, a motorised vent actuator, and a control module configured to open vents when temperature exceeds a threshold

claims.claim_text: An automated greenhouse ventilation system comprising a temperature sensor array, a motorised vent actuator, and a control module configured to open vents when temperature exceeds a threshold

structured_elements.text: control module configured to open vents when temperature exceeds a threshold

claims.accused_product_description: The GreenAir Pro is an automated greenhouse vent controller with three digital temperature sensors, electric linear actuators on the roof vents, and a programmable logic controller that opens vents when temperature exceeds a user-set threshold.

element_mapping_facts.e1: GreenAir Pro is an automated greenhouse ventilation system — matched
element_mapping_facts.e2: Three digital temperature sensors constitute a temperature sensor array — matched
element_mapping_facts.e3: Electric linear actuators on roof vents — satisfies motorised vent actuator — matched
element_mapping_facts.e4: PLC opens vents when temp exceeds threshold — satisfies control module, though term 'module' is disputed

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'control module' in element e4 is disputed — does a general-purpose PLC configured via software qualify as a 'module', or must it be a dedicated hardware controller? The specification describes both PLCs and dedicated microcontrollers as suitable.
claims.specification_notes: The specification describes both dedicated microcontrollers and programmable controllers as suitable implementations of the control module.
